title: Coruscant uprising

{{Quote|Long live the Republic!|Citizen chants during the uprising|<ref name="ROTJ" />}}
An '''uprising''' took place on the city [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and occurred shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="ROTJ" />

==Background==
{{Quote|Palpatine was wrong. Those who live under fear can't wait to throw off that yoke. I've seen the footage taken after the news of the Emperor's death hit the NewsNets. The people of Coruscant celebrated louder and longer than anyone.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="BoS:SotDS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>}}
Following the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|triumph]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and his apprentice [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], riots, uprisings, and celebrations took place on a multitude of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-held worlds, as the news spread across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="ROTJ" /> Despite the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] attempts to prevent the details of the Battle of Endor from becoming public until they could put a proper spin on it, several Rebel groups began transmitting the unaltered facts just hours after the Death Star was destroyed. Although the citizenry of Coruscant were nominally loyal to Palpatine,<ref name="C&tCW">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref><ref name="ROTJ novel">[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars'' Episde VI: ''Return of the Jedi'' novelization]]</ref> there was nonetheless much rejoicing in at least the Monument Plaza.<ref name="ROTJ" />

==The uprising==
On [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], several [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] insurgent groups, including one of which future [[Wraith Squadron]] pilot [[Castin Donn]] was a member, retransmitted the news feed from a [[New Republic HoloNet]] broadcast showing the explosion of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. The rebroadcast overrode the normal channels and went out on a wide band, ensuring that every personal [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] would show the footage.<ref name="XW:IF" />

[[File:Corsectrooper.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A stormtrooper in the CSF is attacked by Rebel-loyal citizens during the uprising.]]

Subsequently, a significant amount of citizens began celebrating the fall of the Empire, and an armed uprising sprang out in several sectors. On [[Monument Plaza/Legends|Monument Plaza]],<ref name="BMF12" /> [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Imperial patrols]] were attacked, statues of Palpatine were toppled with explosives and skimmers and other Imperial symbols were desecrated by Rebels and Rebel-leaning civilians. The citizens started cheering in celebration phrases such as "Down with the Emperor!", "Long live the Republic!", "Freedom!", and at least one Huttese saying of "[[Gi Shatta Gasha/Legends|Gi Shatta Gasha!]]"<ref name="ROTJ" /><ref name="Debunking">{{SWArchive|url=movies/saga/20090204.html|text=Debunking the "Son of Suns" Myth}}</ref> However, in the midst of all the celebrating and rioting, [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] ordered a massive crackdown on the rebellious elements in the city-planet's population and gave full power to the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|local military force]] to end the uprising and restore order to the system. The populace was eventually put back under control through military retaliation, with high death-tolls on the part of the insurrectionists. Donn himself witnessed a young mother being shot in the head and had to grab her infant son to prevent him from being trampled by the fleeing crowd.<ref name="XW:IF" /><ref name="MJ:BTEH" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|By the way, I appreciated the swiftness with which you nabbed the rabble that set off fireworks to mark the Emperor's death.|Sate Pestage to Ysanne Isard|<ref name="MJ:BTEH" />}}
[[File:Coruscant_executions.jpg|250px|thumb|Lighters of fireworks are executed, shortly after the uprising is struck down.]]

On receiving the news that the uprising had been quelled, [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] was glad of the results, although less pleased with the fact that Isard took action without seeking his permission first.<ref name="MJ:BTEH" /> The lack of reaction by the remaining leaders to the revolts likewise convinced Isard that none of them were fit to lead the New Order, and thus engineered the downfall of Pestage, alongside general [[Paltr Carvin]] and [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Bertroff Hissa]], ensuring her role as the unofficial ruler of the Empire by [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Despite the victory and display of power, this was the beginning of the Galactic Empire's end, as feuding [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] and an ever-growing [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] ensured its eventual disintegration into the [[Imperial Remnant]]. [[6 ABY/Legends|Two years]] after the initial uprising was struck down, the New Republic [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|freed the system]] from Imperial control.<ref name="XW:WG">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref> Luke later recalled that the events of the uprising as shown on the HoloNet as well as how the Coruscanti populace in particular celebrated longer and louder than anyone else proved that Palpatine's style of ruling through fear was wrong.<ref name="BoS:SotDS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Coruscant Uprising is somewhat of a retcon maneuver to erase the incompatibility between the celebration scenes inserted in the [[1997]] [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|special edition]] of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] and the notion of the Empire maintaining control of Coruscant for some years after the Battle of Endor. Despite this, however, the event technically was contradicted by Vader's monologue during Palpatine's torture of Luke in the [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]] as well as the sourcebook ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', which implied that the citizenry of Coruscant had genuine loyalty to Palpatine.<ref name="ROTJ novel" /><ref name="C&tCW" />

Contrary to popular belief, the crowd was not shouting "The [[Son of Suns/Legends|Son of Suns]]" during the celebrations. [[Pablo Hidalgo]] verified with [[Matthew Wood]] that the chant in question was actually the Huttese phrase "Gi Shatta Gasha!"<ref name="Debunking" />
